title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
hook: Watch AI-powered characters live, remember, and grow in a simulated town.
promise: You will understand how generative agents create believable, evolving human-like behaviors using memory and reflection.
prerequisite_note: You only need a sense for how computers can simulate simple rules and behaviors.

glossary.plain: A simulated character that acts, thinks, and remembers like a person.
A timeline of experiences and information the agent uses to make decisions.
The process where an agent thinks back on memories to adapt or plan.
How an agent sets goals and organizes steps to achieve them.
A realistic imitation or copy, here of human behavior.
Unexpected, complex actions that arise from simple rules and interactions.
A machine learning system trained to generate and understand human-like text.

cards.title: Simulated People, Real Behaviors
body: You will learn how AI agents can remember, reflect, and plan to act more human.

cards.title: What if AI characters acted like real people?
body: Imagine a digital town filled with AI characters who wake up, make friends, and even host parties, all without scripts.
Generative Agents are AI-powered characters that learn from experience, reflect on their actions, and plan their days, making them surprisingly lifelike.

cards.title: How simulated lives unfold
body: Each agent gathers memories from their daily experiences, storing details about events and people.
They periodically reflect on these memories, drawing insights that shape their future plans and actions.
cards.caption: The cycle of experience, memory, and reflection drives believable agent behavior.

cards.title: Scripted NPCs vs. Generative Agents
body: Toggle to see how traditional game characters compare to memory-driven generative agents.
cards.offNote: Characters repeat fixed routines and cannot adapt to new events.
cards.onNote: Agents adapt, remember past events, and change their plans in response.

cards.title: Memory and reflection fuel believable agents
body: Generative Agents do not just react; they build up a stream of memories as they go about their day.
By reflecting on these memories, agents develop new goals and even change their personalities or social circles over time.

cards.title: How much memory shapes behavior
body: Slide to see how the depth of memory affects agent realism and unpredictability.
cards.low: Agents forget quickly, act repetitively, and seem robotic.
cards.mid: Agents recall recent events, show some adaptation, and act more believably.
cards.high: Agents remember distant events, form rich relationships, and surprise you with complex behaviors.

cards.title: What makes an agent feel real?
body: Pick the six ingredients that together create lifelike generative agents.

choices.label: Reflection
choices.note: Lets the agent learn from the past and adapt.

choices.label: Social Interactions
choices.note: Enables agents to build and change relationships.

choices.label: Simulated Environment
choices.note: Provides a world for agents to act in.

cards.title: Emergent social lives, unscripted
body: Generative Agents hosted parties, formed friendships, and even spread rumors, all without direct programming or scripts.
Observers reported that these agents felt unexpectedly believable and interesting, sometimes even more so than traditional game characters.

bars.label: Believability
bars.note: How real agents seemed to humans

bars.label: Adaptability
bars.note: Ability to change plans or behaviors

cards.caption: Generative agents produced more believable and dynamic behaviors than scripted NPCs.

cards.title: Why Generative Agents changed the field
body: This paper showed that AI agents can go far beyond simple scripts to create social worlds that surprise even their creators.
By combining memory, reflection, and planning, generative agents point toward richer, more human-like simulations in games, research, and beyond.

recap.label: Observe
recap.note: Experience events and interactions in the environment.

recap.label: Remember
recap.note: Store memories of what happened and with whom.

recap.label: Reflect
recap.note: Think back, draw insights, and learn from the past.

recap.label: Plan
recap.note: Set new goals and take actions based on reflection.

cards.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
body: Read the full paper for deeper details and technical implementation.
